header.title: Base64 Encoder/Decoder — Encode & Decode Base64 Online
header.description: Free online Base64 encoder and decoder to encode text or files to Base64 format and decode Base64 strings back to original. Supports text, images, and binary data with live conversion. Ideal for data transmission and web development.
header.keywords: base64 encoder, base64 decoder, encode base64, decode base64, base64 converter, base64 encode online, base64 decode online, base64 string converter

howToUse.title: How to Use the Base64 Encoder/Decoder
howToUse.step1Desc: Select Encode to convert text to Base64, or Decode to convert Base64 back to text.

howToUse.step3Desc: Select Standard, URL-safe, or MIME Base64 variant depending on your use case.
howToUse.step4Desc: The output updates automatically. Click Copy to copy the encoded or decoded result.

items.question: What is Base64 used for?
items.answer: Base64 is commonly used to transmit binary data over text-based protocols like email or HTTP, and to embed images in HTML or CSS.

items.question: Is Base64 encoding the same as encryption?
items.answer: No. Base64 is an encoding scheme, not encryption. It does not provide any security; it only transforms data into a text-safe format.

items.question: What is the difference between standard and URL-safe Base64?
items.answer: URL-safe Base64 replaces + with - and / with _ so the encoded string can be used in URLs and filenames without escaping.

items.question: Does Base64 increase file size?
items.answer: Yes. Base64 encoding increases data size by approximately 33% due to the way binary data is represented as text.

items.question: Can I decode Base64 without internet?
items.answer: Yes. This tool runs entirely in your browser. No data is sent to any server.

header.title: JWT Decoder — Decode & Inspect JWT Tokens Online Free
header.description: Free online JWT decoder to decode and inspect JSON Web Tokens without sending data anywhere. View header, payload, and signature information in a clean interface. Validate token structure and examine claims — 100% client-side.
header.keywords: jwt decoder, decode jwt, jwt inspector, jwt token decoder, json web token decoder, jwt parser, jwt debugger, jwt decode online

JWTDecoderTool.privacyNotice: Your JWT is never sent to any server. All decoding happens in your browser.
JWTDecoderTool.noneAlgorithm: This token uses the 'none' algorithm which provides no security. Never trust tokens signed with 'none'.
JWTDecoderTool.hmacNote: HMAC tokens use a shared secret. The same key is used to sign and verify. Keep it secret!
JWTDecoderTool.signatureNote: Signature verification runs client-side only — this field is for reference.
JWTDecoderTool.cannotVerify: Cannot verify signature client-side without the secret or public key. Decoding only confirms the token is well-formed — not that it's trusted.
JWTDecoderTool.pasteJwt: Paste a JWT token to decode it
JWTDecoderTool.jwtFormat: A JWT has the format xxxxx.yyyyy.zzzzz — three base64url parts separated by dots

howToUse.title: How to Use the JWT Decoder
howToUse.step1Desc: Copy your JWT token and paste it into the input field. The tool auto-detects the token structure.
howToUse.step2Desc: View the Header, Payload, and Signature sections decoded in a readable format.
howToUse.step3Desc: Check standard claims like Issuer, Subject, and Expiration alongside any custom claims.
howToUse.step4Desc: Optionally paste the secret or public key to verify the token signature client-side.

items.question: What is a JWT token?
items.answer: JWT (JSON Web Token) is a compact, URL-safe token format for securely transmitting information as a JSON object.

items.question: Is my JWT data sent to a server?
items.answer: No. All decoding happens entirely in your browser. Your token is never sent to any server.

items.question: What does the signature verify?
items.answer: The signature confirms the token has not been tampered with since it was signed by the issuer.

items.question: What does 'alg: none' mean?
items.answer: It means the token has no signature, which is a security risk. Never trust tokens using the 'none' algorithm.

items.question: What regex engine does this tool use?
items.answer: It uses JavaScript's built-in RegExp engine, supporting standard ECMAScript regex syntax.

items.question: Can I test regex replacement?
items.answer: Yes. Switch to the Replace tab, enter a replacement pattern, and see the result instantly.

items.question: What do the flags mean?
items.answer: Flags modify how the pattern matches: g (global), i (case insensitive), m (multiline), s (dotAll), u (unicode), y (sticky).

items.question: How do I reference captured groups in replacement?
items.answer: Use $1, $2, etc. for numbered groups, $& for the full match, and $` or $' for text before or after the match.

items.question: What is a Unix timestamp?
items.answer: A Unix timestamp is the number of seconds (or milliseconds) that have elapsed since January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC.

items.question: Is there a difference between seconds and milliseconds?
items.answer: Yes. JavaScript's Date.now() returns milliseconds (13 digits), while most APIs use seconds (10 digits).

items.question: Can I see the current Unix timestamp?
items.answer: Yes. The tool displays the current Unix time in real-time at the top of the page.

items.question: Does this support time zones?
items.answer: Yes. You can view results in both your local time zone and UTC, along with ISO 8601 and RFC 2822 formats.

items.question: What is URL encoding?
items.answer: URL encoding converts special characters into a percent-sign (%) followed by two hex digits so they can be safely transmitted in URLs.

items.question: When should I use URL Component vs Full URL?
items.answer: Use URL Component to encode only a query parameter value. Use Full URL to encode an entire URL safely.

items.question: Why does a space encode as + or %20?
items.answer: application/x-www-form-urlencoded encodes spaces as +, while URL percent-encoding uses %20. Both are valid depending on context.

items.question: Can I decode a URL that I encoded?
items.answer: Yes. Switch to Decode mode and paste the encoded string to get back the original text.

items.question: What is the difference between UUID v4 and v7?
items.answer: UUID v4 is randomly generated, while UUID v7 is time-ordered, making it better for database indexing.

items.question: What is a ULID?
items.answer: ULID is a lexicographically sortable unique identifier that is URL-safe and case-insensitive.

items.question: Can I generate multiple IDs at once?

items.question: What is NanoID?
items.answer: NanoID is a compact, URL-safe unique identifier that is shorter than UUID and customizable in length.

header.title: Grayscale Converter — Turn Images to Black & White Online
header.description: Free online grayscale converter to turn your images into black and white. Choose from multiple grayscale algorithms including luminosity, average, and desaturation. Adjust contrast and brightness for the perfect monochrome effect — 100% client-side.
header.keywords: grayscale converter, convert to grayscale, black and white converter, image to grayscale, grayscale image, black and white photo converter, desaturate image, grayscale filter

dropzone.clickOrDrag: Click or drag & drop an image
dropzone.formats: JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF · Processed locally

GrayscaleConverterTool.privacy: Your image never leaves your device.

howToUse.title: How to Use Grayscale Converter
howToUse.step1Desc: Drag and drop an image or click to browse from your device.
howToUse.step2Desc: Select from Luminance, Average, Lightness, or Intensity algorithms.
howToUse.step3Desc: Adjust contrast and brightness for the perfect monochrome effect.
howToUse.step4Desc: Click Convert to Grayscale, compare with the original, then download.

items.question: What is the difference between grayscale methods?
items.answer: Luminance weights colors by human perception, Average takes a simple mean, Lightness uses the midpoint of RGB extremes, and Intensity uses a fixed weighted formula.

items.question: Can I adjust contrast and brightness?
items.answer: Yes. Use the sliders to fine-tune contrast and brightness before converting.

items.question: Does this work on any image format?
items.answer: Yes. We support JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, and other common formats.

items.question: Is the processing done locally?
items.answer: Yes. Your image never leaves your device — all processing is done in your browser.

items.question: Can I save in different formats?
items.answer: Yes. You can choose the output format and quality before downloading.

items.question: What is the best method for photos?
items.answer: The Luminance method is generally best for photos as it accounts for how humans perceive brightness.

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.sampleText: Convert a color portrait to a classic black and white photo using the Luminance method.
examples.result: A natural-looking black and white photo that preserves human-perceived brightness.

header.title: Image Compressor — Reduce Image File Size Online Free
header.description: Free online image compressor to reduce image file size while preserving quality. Compress JPG, PNG, WebP, and GIF images with adjustable compression levels. Optimize images for web, email, and storage — 100% client-side.
header.keywords: image compressor, compress image, reduce image size, image optimizer, compress jpg, compress png, image file size reducer, photo compressor

dropzone.formatsSupported: JPG, PNG, WebP · Multiple files supported

options.title: Compression Options
options.targetSizeDesc: Uses binary search to find the best quality that fits the target.

howToUse.title: How to Use Image Compressor
howToUse.step1Desc: Drag and drop one or more images onto the compressor.
howToUse.step2Desc: Select By Quality for direct control or By Size to target a specific file size.
howToUse.step3Desc: Set quality level, output format, max dimensions, and strip metadata if needed.
howToUse.step4Desc: Click Compress All, compare sizes, then download individually or as a zip.

items.question: How much can I compress an image?
items.answer: It depends on the image content and format. Typically you can reduce file size by 50-80% without noticeable quality loss.

items.question: What is the difference between By Quality and By Size?
items.answer: By Quality lets you set a fixed quality level. By Size uses binary search to find the optimal quality that meets your target file size.

items.question: Does compression reduce image dimensions?
items.answer: By default, no. But you can enable the Limit Width option to resize during compression.

items.question: What does Strip Metadata do?
items.answer: It removes EXIF data, camera info, and other metadata from the image to reduce file size.

items.question: Can I compress multiple images at once?
items.answer: Yes. Upload multiple images and compress them all in one click.

items.question: Is the compression lossless?
items.answer: Lossy compression reduces quality. For maximum quality, use high quality settings or lossless formats like PNG.

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.sampleText: Compress a high-res photo to 80% quality for fast web loading.
examples.result: A dramatically smaller file with minimal visible quality loss.

header.title: Image Flipper — Flip Photos Horizontally or Vertically Online
header.description: Free online image flipper to flip photos horizontally or vertically with a single click. Mirror images for perfect reflections, reverse selfies, or create symmetrical compositions — 100% client-side, your images stay private.
header.keywords: image flipper, flip image, flip photo, mirror image, horizontal flip, vertical flip, photo flipper, mirror photo online

dropzone.clickOrDrag: Click or drag & drop your image

howToUse.step4Desc: Click Flip Image, preview the result, then download.

items.question: What is the difference between horizontal and vertical flip?
items.answer: Horizontal flip mirrors the image left-to-right. Vertical flip mirrors it top-to-bottom.

items.question: Does flipping change image dimensions?
items.answer: No. Flipping preserves the original dimensions and resolution.

items.question: Can I flip multiple images at once?
items.answer: This tool processes one image at a time. For batch flipping, process images individually.

items.question: What formats are supported?
items.answer: We support JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, and BMP.

items.question: Is the processing done locally?
items.answer: Yes. Your image never leaves your device.

items.question: Can I flip both horizontally and vertically at once?
items.answer: Yes. Select the Both option to flip on both axes simultaneously.

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.sampleText: Flip a selfie horizontally to correct the mirror image effect.
examples.result: A correctly oriented selfie with no mirror reversal.

header.title: Image Merger — Combine Images Horizontally, Vertically & Grid
header.description: Free online image merger to combine multiple images into a single picture. Stack images horizontally, vertically, or arrange them in a custom grid layout. Adjust spacing, alignment, and background color — 100% client-side, nothing uploaded.

howToUse.step3Desc: Set gap size, alignment, background color, and grid columns if using Grid layout.
howToUse.step4Desc: Click Merge Images, then download the combined single image.

items.question: How many images can I merge?
items.answer: You can merge as many images as you need. There is no hard limit.

items.question: What layout options are available?
items.answer: You can merge images horizontally, vertically, or in a grid layout with configurable columns.

items.question: Can I adjust spacing between images?
items.answer: Yes. Use the Gap setting to add or remove space between images.

items.question: What happens if images have different sizes?
items.answer: You can choose an alignment mode to control how differently-sized images are positioned.

items.question: Can I set a background color?
items.answer: Yes. Choose any background color for areas not covered by images.

items.question: Is my data safe?
items.answer: Yes. All processing happens locally in your browser — nothing is uploaded.

header.title: Image Resizer — Resize Photos by Pixels or Percentage Online
header.description: Free online image resizer to resize photos by exact pixels, percentage, or preset dimensions. Maintain aspect ratio or crop freely to fit your needs. Perfect for social media, websites, and printing — 100% client-side.
header.keywords: image resizer, resize image, resize photo, image resizing tool, photo resizer, change image dimensions, resize picture online, image scaler

privacy.localProcessing: Your image never leaves your device — 100% local processing.

howToUse.title: How to Use Image Resizer
howToUse.step1Desc: Drag and drop an image or click to browse from your device.
howToUse.step2Desc: Select pixel dimensions, percentage scale, or a preset size.
howToUse.step3Desc: Enter width and height. Lock aspect ratio to maintain proportions.
howToUse.step4Desc: Click Resize Image, then download your resized image in the chosen format.

items.question: Can I resize by percentage?
items.answer: Yes. Switch to Percentage mode to resize by a percentage of the original dimensions.

items.question: What is the aspect ratio lock?
items.answer: When locked, changing one dimension automatically adjusts the other to maintain the original proportions.

items.question: Are there preset sizes?
items.answer: Yes. Choose from presets like HD, Full HD, 4K, Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, avatar, and more.

items.question: Does resizing affect image quality?
items.answer: Resizing to smaller dimensions reduces detail. The quality setting also affects the output file size and appearance.

items.question: What is Fit Mode?
items.answer: Fit Mode controls how the image is fitted into the specified dimensions — such as contain, cover, or fill.

items.question: What formats can I save to?
items.answer: You can save the resized image as JPG, PNG, WebP, or other supported formats.

items.question: Is the processing done locally?
items.answer: Yes. Your image never leaves your device — 100% local processing.

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.sampleText: Resize a photo to 1080×1080px for Instagram square posts.

dropzone.fileTypes: JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, BMP · Processed locally

warnings.jpegNoTransparency: JPEG doesn't support transparency — use PNG or WebP for transparent backgrounds.

howToUse.title: How to Use Image Rotator
howToUse.step1Desc: Drag and drop an image or click to browse from your device.
howToUse.step2Desc: Select a preset angle (90°, 180°, 270°) or enter a custom angle.
howToUse.step3Desc: Choose a background fill color and select output format and quality.
howToUse.step4Desc: Click Rotate Image, then download your rotated image.

items.question: Can I rotate by a custom angle?
items.answer: Yes. Enter any custom angle between -360 and 360 degrees.

items.question: What happens to the background after rotation?
items.answer: Rotated images may have transparent or blank areas. You can fill these with a solid color or leave them transparent.

items.question: Does rotation affect image quality?
items.answer: 90°, 180°, and 270° rotations are lossless. Custom angles may require interpolation which can slightly affect quality.

items.question: Can I flip and rotate at the same time?
items.answer: This tool focuses on rotation. Use the Image Flipper tool for flipping operations.

items.question: What formats are supported?
items.answer: We support JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, and BMP.

items.question: Why does JPEG not support transparency?
items.answer: JPEG format does not support alpha channels. Use PNG or WebP if you need transparent backgrounds.

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.sampleText: Correct a photo taken upside down by rotating it 180°.
examples.result: An upright photo with correct orientation, ready to download.

header.title: Image to Base64 — Encode & Decode Base64 Images Online
header.description: Free online image to Base64 converter to encode images into Base64 strings and decode Base64 strings back to images. Supports JPG, PNG, GIF, and WebP. Ideal for web developers embedding images directly in HTML, CSS, or JavaScript — 100% client-side.
header.keywords: image to base64, base64 encoder, base64 decoder, convert image to base64, base64 image converter, image encoder, base64 string generator, image to base64 online

howToUse.title: How to Use Image to Base64
howToUse.step1Desc: Select Encode to convert an image to Base64, or Decode to convert Base64 back to an image.
howToUse.step2Desc: Drag and drop an image to encode, or paste a Base64 string to decode.
howToUse.step3Desc: Choose the image format for the encoded output or download the decoded image.
howToUse.step4Desc: Copy the Base64 string to your clipboard and embed it in HTML, CSS, or JavaScript.

items.question: What is Base64 encoding?
items.answer: Base64 is an encoding method that converts binary data (like images) into a text string of ASCII characters.

items.question: When should I use Base64 images?
items.answer: Base64 is useful for embedding small images directly in HTML, CSS, or JavaScript to reduce HTTP requests.

items.question: Does Base64 increase file size?
items.answer: Yes. Base64 encoding increases the size by approximately 33% compared to the original binary file.

items.question: Can I decode a Base64 string back to an image?
items.answer: Yes. Switch to the Decode tab, paste the Base64 string, and click Decode to Image.

items.question: What image formats are supported?
items.answer: We support JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, and SVG for encoding and decoding.

items.question: Is my image data private?
items.answer: Yes. All processing happens locally in your browser — nothing is uploaded to any server.

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.sampleText: Encode a small icon as Base64 and embed it directly in HTML using a data URL.
examples.result: A complete data URL string ready to paste into your HTML src attribute.

metadata.title: About Us — Free Online Tools Built for Everyone | Fancy Toolbox
metadata.description: Learn about Fancy Toolbox: a privacy-first, browser-based suite of 50+ free online tools. No sign-up, no uploads — every tool runs locally on your device.
metadata.keywords: about fancy toolbox, free online tools, browser-based tools, privacy tools

sections.heading: Our Mission
sections.body: Fancy Toolbox is a comprehensive collection of free online tools designed to help you get things done quickly and efficiently. From text manipulation to image editing, PDF processing to developer utilities — we've got you covered.

sections.heading: Why Fancy Toolbox?
sections.body: We believe that powerful tools should be accessible to everyone. That's why all our tools are completely free, work directly in your browser, and require no sign-up or personal information. Your privacy is our priority — all processing happens locally on your device.

sections.heading: What Makes Us Different
sections.body: Our tools are built with modern web technologies, ensuring fast performance and a seamless user experience across all devices. We're constantly adding new tools and improving existing ones based on user feedback.

metadata.title: All Free Online Tools — 50+ Browser-Based Utilities | Fancy Toolbox
metadata.description: Browse 50+ free online tools at Fancy Toolbox. Word counter, image resizer, PDF merger, JSON formatter, unit converter, password generator & more. No sign-up, works in your browser.
metadata.keywords: all tools, free online tools, browser tools, word counter, pdf merger, image resizer, json formatter

metadata.title: Changelog — What's New at Fancy Toolbox | Product Updates
metadata.description: Stay up to date with the latest Fancy Toolbox updates. See new tools, feature improvements, bug fixes, and product announcements all in one place.
metadata.keywords: changelog, product updates, new tools, feature releases, fancy toolbox updates

sections.heading: Initial Release
sections.body: We're excited to launch Fancy Toolbox with a comprehensive collection of free online tools including text manipulation, image editing, PDF processing, developer utilities, math converters, and security tools. All tools run entirely in your browser with no sign-up required.

metadata.title: Contact Us — Get in Touch with the Fancy Toolbox Team
metadata.description: Have a question, suggestion, or bug report? Contact the Fancy Toolbox team. We'd love to hear your feedback about our free online tools.
metadata.keywords: contact fancy toolbox, support, feedback, bug report, feature request

content.title: Contact Us
content.subtitle: We'd love to hear from you

sections.heading: Get in Touch
sections.body: Have a question, suggestion, or feedback? We'd love to hear from you. Whether it's a bug report, feature request, or just a friendly hello — don't hesitate to reach out.

sections.heading: Report an Issue
sections.body: If you've encountered a bug or something isn't working as expected, please let us know. Include as much detail as possible, such as the tool you were using, your browser, and steps to reproduce the issue.

sections.heading: Feature Requests
sections.body: Can't find a tool you need? Let us know what you'd like to see added to Fancy Toolbox. We're always looking for ideas to make our platform more useful.

metadata.title: Cookie Policy — How Fancy Toolbox Uses Cookies
metadata.description: Fancy Toolbox uses minimal essential cookies only — no tracking, no advertising, no third-party cookies. Learn how we manage cookies on our site.
metadata.keywords: cookie policy, essential cookies, no tracking cookies, browser cookies, privacy

sections.heading: What Are Cookies
sections.body: Cookies are small text files stored on your device by your web browser. They are widely used to make websites work efficiently and provide information to site owners.

sections.heading: How We Use Cookies
sections.body: Fancy Toolbox uses minimal cookies that are strictly necessary for the website to function. We do not use tracking cookies, advertising cookies, or any third-party cookies that collect personal information.

sections.heading: Essential Cookies
sections.body: We may use essential cookies to remember your language preference and theme selection (light/dark mode). These cookies are necessary for the site to function properly and do not collect any personal data.

sections.heading: Managing Cookies
sections.body: You can control and manage cookies in your browser settings. Please note that disabling certain cookies may affect the functionality of the website.

sections.heading: Updates
sections.body: We may update this cookie policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page.

metadata.title: Popular Free Online Tools — Most Used Browser Utilities | Fancy Toolbox
metadata.description: Discover the most popular free online tools handpicked from our collection. Word counter, image resizer, PDF merger, JSON formatter, password generator & more trending tools used daily.
metadata.keywords: popular tools, trending tools, most used tools, word counter, image resizer, pdf merger, password generator

content.title: Popular Tools
content.subtitle: Most used tools by our community

metadata.title: Privacy Policy — How Fancy Toolbox Protects Your Data
metadata.description: Your privacy matters. Read Fancy Toolbox's privacy policy: no data collection, no file uploads, all processing happens locally in your browser. Zero tracking, total privacy.
metadata.keywords: privacy policy, data protection, online privacy, no tracking, browser-based processing

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.body: At Fancy Toolbox, your privacy is our top priority. This Privacy Policy outlines how we collect, use, and protect your information when you use our website and tools.

sections.heading: Data Collection
sections.body: We do not collect, store, or transmit any personal data. All tools run entirely in your browser — your files, text, and content never leave your device. We do not use cookies for tracking purposes.

sections.heading: Analytics
sections.body: We use minimal, privacy-focused analytics to understand general usage patterns. This data is anonymized and cannot be used to identify individual users. No personal information is shared with third parties.

sections.heading: Third-Party Services
sections.body: We do not sell, trade, or transfer your information to third parties. Our website may contain links to external sites, but we are not responsible for their privacy practices.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
sections.body: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: If you have any questions about this privacy policy, please contact us at contact@fancytoolbox.com.

metadata.title: Terms of Service — Rules & Guidelines | Fancy Toolbox
metadata.description: Read the Terms of Service for Fancy Toolbox. By using our free online tools, you agree to these guidelines. Tools provided 'as is' without warranty.
metadata.keywords: terms of service, terms and conditions, user agreement, fancy toolbox terms

sections.heading: Acceptance of Terms
sections.body: By accessing or using Fancy Toolbox, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use our services.

sections.heading: Use of Service
sections.body: Our tools are provided for lawful purposes only. You agree not to misuse the services for any illegal or unauthorized purpose. All tools are provided 'as is' without any warranty.

sections.heading: Intellectual Property
sections.body: The content, design, and code of Fancy Toolbox are owned by us and protected by applicable intellectual property laws. You may not reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works without our prior written consent.

sections.heading: Limitation of Liability
sections.body: Fancy Toolbox and its creators shall not be liable for any damages arising from the use or inability to use our services. We make no guarantees about the accuracy, reliability, or availability of the tools.

sections.heading: Changes to Terms
sections.body: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
